name: Eledon Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
business: Eledon is a clinical stage biotechnology company using our immunology expertise in targeting the CD40 Ligand (CD40L) pathway to develop therapies to protect transplanted organs and prevent rejection, and to treat amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS).

exposures.sentence: Our lead compound in development is tegoprubart, an IgG1, anti-CD40L antibody with high affinity for the CD40L, a well-validated biological target that we believe has broad therapeutic potential.

catalysts.event: Announced topline data from Phase 2 BESTOW trial
catalysts.sentence: On November 6, 2025, we announced topline efficacy and safety data from the Phase 2 BESTOW trial evaluating tegoprubart for the prevention of kidney transplant rejection.

catalysts.event: Presented 24-month follow-up data from Phase 1b long-term extension
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we presented 24-month follow-up data from eight patients enrolled in the Phase 1b trial long-term extension evaluating tegoprubart in kidney transplantation at the American Society of Transplant Surgeons Winter Symposium held in Scottsdale, Arizona.

catalysts.event: Plan to seek FDA guidance for Phase 3 trial design
catalysts.sentence: In 2026, we plan to seek guidance from the FDA regarding the design of a potential Phase 3 clinical trial evaluating tegoprubart in kidney transplantation and, subject to regulatory feedback and alignment, may initiate a Phase 3 trial.

tamClaims.claim: There are an estimated 27,000 kidneys transplanted annually in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: There are an estimated 27,000 kidneys transplanted annually in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: In the U.S., the incidence is estimated at approximately 5,000 cases per year with a prevalence of approximately 30,000 cases overall.
tamClaims.sentence: In the U.S., the incidence is estimated at approximately 5,000 cases per year with a prevalence of approximately 30,000 cases overall.

tamClaims.claim: Type 1 diabetes (T1D) ... affects approximately 2 million persons in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: Type 1 diabetes (T1D) is a T cell mediated autoimmune disease with progressive loss of insulin producing pancreatic beta cells and affects approximately 2 million persons in the United States.
